response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer with broad experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and his C++ exposure is limited to a six-month contract at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing IDE components — not financial systems, not Windows-platform production C++. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience evident, no fixed-income or capital-markets domain knowledge, and no exposure to relevant tooling like Summit, Kafka, or messaging frameworks common in banking infrastructure. RIDE Capital is the only finance-adjacent employer, but the work there was Node/TypeScript/GraphQL for a retail investment startup, not capital-markets engineering.
strengths: Strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) demonstrated consistently across multiple roles
Broad polyglot background including Java and some C++ experience, showing capacity to work across languages
Proven ability to handle high-throughput, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (6 months, 2014–2015); no Windows-platform C++ production work anywhere in the resume
Zero fixed-income, derivatives, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain knowledge
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL appears only as a secondary skill with no substantive project evidence
